items.headline: ISRO completes crew module system tests for Gaganyaan mission
items.title: ISRO successfully carries out tests of Gaganyaan crew module systems - The Hindu
items.scifi_hook: India readies its first crewed spacecraft after successful module trials.
items.eli5: India's space agency tested the life‑support and control systems for its upcoming human flight.
items.why: Validates critical hardware for the nation's first crewed orbital mission.
items.text: ISRO successfully carries out tests of Gaganyaan crew module systems The Hindu

items.headline: China adopts methalox Long March 10C as commercial launch workhorse
items.title: China lines up methalox Long March 10C as commercial workhorse after first booster recovery
items.scifi_hook: China's new methane‑fuel rocket returns its booster and targets commercial payloads.
items.eli5: China plans to use a reusable rocket that burns methane to launch satellites for paying customers.
items.why: Shows China's push for reusable, cost‑effective commercial launch capability.
items.text: HELSINKI — China confirmed a methalox Long March 10C as its commercial workhorse following its first successful booster recovery, while injecting fresh capital into commercial rocket ventures. The China Academy […] The post China lines up methalox Long March 10C as commercial workhorse after first booster recovery appeared first on SpaceNews .

items.headline: SpaceX schedules Starship Flight 13 for mid‑July
items.title: SpaceX gears up for Starship Flight 13
items.scifi_hook: SpaceX aims to launch its massive Starship again, carrying fresh Starlink satellites.
items.eli5: SpaceX plans to fly its giant rocket to test fixes and put more internet satellites into orbit.
items.why: Advances reusable heavy‑lift launch and global broadband satellite deployment.
items.text: SpaceX plans to conduct its next Starship launch as soon as July 16 to test fixes to issues from the previous flight and deploy functioning Starlink satellites. The post SpaceX gears up for Starship Flight 13 appeared first on SpaceNews .

items.headline: New copper‑catalysed method adds cyanide to primary amines
items.title: Direct copper-catalysed deaminative cyanation of aliphatic primary amines - Nature
items.scifi_hook: Scientists use copper to directly attach cyanide groups to simple amines in one step.
items.eli5: A copper catalyst lets chemists turn basic nitrogen compounds into useful cyanide‑containing chemicals quickly.
items.why: Could streamline production of pharmaceuticals and agrochemicals.
items.text: Direct copper-catalysed deaminative cyanation of aliphatic primary amines Nature

items.headline: First superconducting quantum heat engine demonstrated
items.title: World's first superconducting quantum heat engine offers path to larger quantum computers
items.scifi_hook: A Finnish team built a tiny engine that runs on quantum heat cycles inside a superconducting chip.
items.eli5: Researchers created a tiny engine that uses quantum rules to turn heat into work on a superconducting circuit.
items.why: Shows a new way to manage energy in future quantum computers.
items.text: Recent improvements in our understanding of how the principles of thermodynamics apply in the quantum realm could give a boost to quantum technology, and a clearer picture of quantum thermodynamics could in turn enhance our understanding of classical thermodynamics. Now, Aalto University researchers have demonstrated the first cyclic quantum heat engine inside a superconducting circuit.

items.headline: RNA‑only repair enzyme sheds light on early genome protection
items.title: RNA-only repair enzyme reveals how primordial life could have protected genomes
items.scifi_hook: A primitive enzyme that fixes RNA hints how the first life forms guarded their genetic code.
items.eli5: Scientists found an enzyme that can repair RNA without proteins, showing a possible ancient way cells fixed their genes.
items.why: Offers clues to the evolution of molecular biology and new biotech tools.
items.text: In most modern cells, DNA stores the genetic blueprint, and proteins replicate, repair and build from those blueprints. At the same time, proteins require instructions from DNA to be made in the first place.

items.headline: Solar installations outpace coal worldwide in 2026
items.title: Solar surpasses coal globally as US installations keep climbing in 2026 - MarketScale
items.scifi_hook: Global solar power capacity now exceeds coal, driven by rapid U.S. growth.
items.eli5: More solar panels were installed worldwide this year than coal plants, especially in the United States.
items.why: Marks a shift toward renewable electricity generation on a global scale.
items.text: Solar surpasses coal globally as US installations keep climbing in 2026 MarketScale
